summary: One-file lbcollision matching pass focused on capsule/capsule collision math. The PR renamed and documented the 3D capsule solver lbColl_80006E58 by hit/hurt roles, rewrote closest-point and broadphase locals to improve MWCC register/stack behavior, removed the local lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr wrapper in favor of HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr, factored repeated debug drawing into lbColl_DrawHitResult, and improved multiple match percentages. Four draw-related functions reached 100%; lbColl_80006094, lbColl_800067F8, and lbColl_80006E58 remained below 100% due to residual float-register-allocation mismatches.
key_files: src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c (+670/-621, 50 hunks): capsule collision solver, 2D/3D closest-point math, hurt/hit debug draw helpers
what_changed: lbColl_80006E58 was given semantic parameters and comments: hit_start/hit_end, hurt_start/hurt_end, hit_closest/hurt_closest, hurt_mtx, out_contact_pos, out_overlap, hit_radius, hurt_radius, broadphase_scale.
lbColl_80006E58 call sites were updated for the new argument order, moving out_overlap before hit_radius/hurt_radius/broadphase_scale.

The 3D capsule narrow-phase in lbColl_80006E58 was rewritten around named broadphase_radius, segment_dot, hit_len_sq, hurt_len_sq, closest_denom, hit_param, hurt_param, closest_dist, local_dist, and related local-space radius calculations.
The custom static inline lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr and its lbColl_804D3700/lbColl_804D3708 assert strings were removed; call sites now use HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr.
Added lbColl_DrawHitResult to share the HSD_MtxInverse + PSMTXMultVec + lbColl_800096B4 path used by several hit/hurt debug draw functions.
Four functions became 100% matches: lbColl_8000A244, lbColl_8000A78C, lbColl_8000A95C, and lbColl_8000AB2C.
Unmatched functions improved: lbColl_80006094 78.7% to 91.4%, lbColl_800067F8 78.7% to 89.4%, lbColl_80006E58 81.0% to 95.4%, and lbColl_8000A584 99.7% to 99.8%.

lbColl_80005C44 was cleaned up with scoped dot/scale locals, literal clamp values, and SQ() usage; an attempted Vec3 local reshuffle caused a temporary 99.99% regression and was fixed by restoring the stack-sensitive local layout.

lbColl_80006094 and lbColl_800067F8 received stack-padding/local-scope reshuffles, Vec3 copies in selected branches, approximatelyZero calls, and explicit lbColl_804D79F8/lbColl_804D7A08/lbColl_804D7A10/lbColl_804D7A18 constants for clamp and midpoint behavior.
smart_moves: Naming lbColl_80006E58 by collision role made a long, pointer-heavy signature safer to reason about and made call-site comments self-documenting.
Deleting lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr removed a duplicate JObj matrix accessor and unused assert strings instead of preserving dead matching scaffolding.
Factoring lbColl_DrawHitResult avoided repeating inverse-transform draw setup and helped several near-matching debug draw functions reach 100%.
The closest-point solve kept both hit_param and hit_param_candidate live with an assignment expression, a targeted tactic to avoid redundant float moves.
Caching the 1.0 constant in a callee-save float for repeated *_param = 1.0 branches directly targeted float register allocation.

The PR used named geometry concepts such as broadphase_radius, closest_denom, local_dist_sq, scaled_hurt_radius, contact_lerp, and out_overlap rather than leaving the 3D capsule solve as temp_f-style math.
decomp_lessons: Do not assume converting float x/y/z triplets into Vec3 is free: the merge notes say moving Vec3 locals and replacing a three-float d2 triplet in lbColl_80005C44 expanded the frame by 8 bytes and shifted stack offsets, breaking a 100% match by one byte.
For stack-sensitive functions, preserve declaration order and local shape even when a Vec3 abstraction is semantically cleaner.
Scoped locals and extra braces can be useful MWCC controls: this PR repeatedly used block scope to affect lifetimes, stack slots, and float register allocation.
Use exact constants according to the match site: some functions matched better with lbColl_804D* constants, while lbColl_80005C44 cleanup used literal 0.0f/1.0f and SQ().
Automated decomp.dev reports are important regression checks; the bot caught lbColl_80005C44 falling from 100.00% to 99.99%, and the final history documents restoring the local layout.
Residual mismatch notes are valuable: the PR explicitly left lbColl_80006094, lbColl_800067F8, and lbColl_80006E58 below 100% because remaining issues were float-register-allocation mismatches.
naming_conventions: Use hit_start and hit_end for the hit capsule axis endpoints.
Use hurt_start and hurt_end for hurt/shield capsule axis endpoints.
Use hit_closest and hurt_closest for closest points produced by segment/capsule solves.
Use hurt_mtx for the matrix used to evaluate hurt radius in local space.
Use out_contact_pos and out_overlap for collision result outputs.
Use hit_radius, hurt_radius, and broadphase_scale for radius inputs instead of generic scl/arg10/arg11.
Use *_param for segment interpolation factors and candidate_*_param for projected alternatives.
Use *_delta, *_len_sq, *_dist_sq, closest_denom, and broadphase_radius for collision geometry quantities.
Use is_* flags for degenerate, parallel, and zero-distance cases.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK was adjusted in the heavily unmatched functions, including lbColl_80006094 from 56 to 80, lbColl_800067F8 from 24 to 72, and lbColl_80006E58 from 112 to 64.
Use of assignment expression hit_param = (hit_param_candidate = ...) was intentional to keep both float values live without an extra fmr.
A reused local was used to cache lbColl_804D7A08/1.0 across several hit_param and hurt_param clamp branches.

Vec3 temporaries were introduced selectively in parallel-axis branches where the assembly appeared to treat x/y/z as a unit, but this tactic was rolled back for lbColl_80005C44 where it hurt stack layout.
Volatile float stack temporaries were used around frsqrte/Newton distance calculations in lbColl_80006E58 to preserve store/reload and rounding behavior.
Several nearzero calls in lbColl_80006094 and lbColl_800067F8 were replaced with the existing approximatelyZero helper.
The local draw helper lbColl_DrawHitResult changed repeated inline inverse-transform code into one shared inline-like pattern that matched multiple 99.9% functions.
SQ() was used in lbColl_80005C44 to express repeated multiplication in the form that matched.
review_feedback: No formal reviews or inline review comments were present in the slice.
The author commented that the PR was opened prematurely.
decomp.dev reported matched code 67.68% (+0.03%, +1300 bytes), four new matches, and one broken match: lbColl_80005C44 fell from 100.00% to 99.99%.
The author replied that they would fix the regressions.

The final merge history documents the lbColl_80005C44 regression fix: restore the original declaration order and three-float d2 locals because the Vec3 reshuffle changed the stack frame from -0x48 to -0x50.
The author noted an editorconfig check could not be re-run locally because of a network error while downloading nix packages.
follow_up_queries: Search later lbcollision PRs for lbColl_80006E58 float register allocation fixes after 95.4%.
Investigate remaining mismatches in lbColl_80006094 and lbColl_800067F8, especially clamp branches and approximatelyZero/constant choices.
When proposing Vec3 cleanup in matched functions, verify stack frame size and local offset stability before merging.
Check whether the added static inline between helper is used in later closest-point solve cleanup or can be removed/applied.
Look for other local wrappers around HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr that can be removed without changing matching.
Compare subsequent changes to lbColl_DrawHitResult and the debug draw functions that reached 100% in this PR.
